blocks:
Sūrat al-A‘lā

Meccan and it is said Medinan, and it consists of nineteen verses by consensus بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ الْأَعْلَى (1) Glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Exalted, Al-Qummi said: Say: سبحان ربّي الأعلى. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When you recite سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ الْأَعْلَى, then say سبحان ربّي الأعلى, and if you are in the prayer (الصلاة), say it between yourself and your soul. And from Ibn Abbas: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, when He recited Surah سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ الْأَعْلَى, would say سبحان ربّي الأعلى. And likewise, it is narrated from Ali (peace be upon him). And in al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani, he said: When فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ was revealed, the Messenger of Allah said: "Place it in your bowing (ركوع)." And when سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ الْأَعْلَى was revealed, He said: "Place it in your prostration (سجود)." فَسَبِّحْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الْعَظِيمِ (So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Great,) It was said: And they used to say during the bowing (ركوع): "O Allah, for You I have bowed (ركعت)," and during the prostration (سجود): "O Allah, for You I have prostrated (سجدت)."

(٢) الَّذِي خَلَقَ فَسَوَّى ** (2) Who created and proportioned, He created everything and proportioned His creation by making for it that through which its perfection is attained and its sustenance (معاشه) is completed. (٣) وَالَّذِي قَدَّرَ فَهَدى ** (3) and Who determined and guided, Al-Qummi said: He determined (قدّر) things with the first determination (التقدير الأوّل), then He guided to them whomever He wills. And it was recited قدر (قدَر) with the lightening (بالتخفيف) [of the dāl]. And in al-Majma': It is the recitation (قراءة) of Ali (peace be upon him). (٤) وَالَّذِي أَخْرَجَ الْمَرْعى ** (4) and Who brought forth the pasture, Al-Qummi said: Meaning, the vegetation (النّبات). (٥) فَجَعَلَهُ ** (5) and then turned it into a blackish chaff. غُثاءً أَحْوى ** after bringing it forth, meaning dry and black. Al-Qummi said: It becomes brittle (هشيماً) after reaching its maturity and turns black (ويسودّ). (٦) سَنُقْرِئُكَ ** (6) Soon We shall make you recite, and you will not forget, He said: Meaning, We shall teach you (نعلّمك) فَلا تَنْسى. (and you will not forget). (٧) إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ ** (7) except what Allah wills. Al-Qummi said: Then He made an exception (استثنى) because forgetfulness (النّسيان) is not guaranteed against him (the Prophet), for the One Who does not forget is Allah. And in al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas, he said: When Gabriel descended upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with the revelation (بالوحي), he would recite it for fear that he might forget it. Gabriel would not finish the end of the revelation until he (the Prophet) spoke its beginning. When this verse was revealed, he did not forget anything after that. إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ وَما يَخْفى ** (Indeed He knows the manifest and what is hidden) What is manifest (ما ظهر) of your states (أحوالكم) and what is hidden (وما بطن).

(٨) وَنُيَسِّرُكَ لِلْيُسْرى ** (8) and We shall ease you into the easiest way. The easiest way (الطريقة اليسرى) in preserving the revelation (الوحي). (1) (٩) فَذَكِّرْ إِنْ نَفَعَتِ الذِّكْرى. (9) So admonish, if the admonition is beneficial. (١٠) سَيَذَّكَّرُ مَنْ يَخْشى ** (10) Soon he who fears [Allah] will be admonished, He who fears Allah will be admonished and benefit from it. (١١) وَيَتَجَنَّبُهَا ** (11) but the most wretched will avoid it, And the admonition (الذكرى) will be avoided الْأَشْقَى. (11) the most wretched. (١٢) الَّذِي يَصْلَى النَّارَ الْكُبْرى ** (12) he who shall enter the great Fire, Al-Qummi said: The Fire of the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). (١٣) ثُمَّ لا يَمُوتُ فِيها ** (13) where he will neither die So that he may rest وَلا يَحْيى ** (13) nor live, a life that benefits him, thus being as Allah said: وَيَأْتِيهِ الْمَوْتُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ وَما هُوَ بِمَيِّتٍ. (17) and death will come upon him from every side, but he will not die. (١٤) قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ تَزَكَّى ** (14) Certainly, successful is he who purifies himself, He purified himself from polytheism (الشّرك) and disobedience (المعصية). (١٥) وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ ** (15) and remembers the Name of his Lord, With his heart and his tongue فَصَلَّى ** (15) and prays. Al-Qummi said regarding قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ تَزَكَّى : He said it is Zakat al-Fitr (زكاة الفطر) when one gives it out before the Eid prayer (صلاة العيد). وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّى : He said it is the prayer of Fitr (صلاة الفطر) and Adha (الأضحى). In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked concerning the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ (14) Certainly, successful is he who تَزَكَّى ** (14) purifies himself. He said: "He who gives out the Fitrah (الفطرة)." He was asked concerning وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّى ** (15) and remembers the Name of his Lord, and prays. He said: "He went out to the open ground (الجبانة) and prayed." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He said to a man: "What is the meaning of the Almighty's saying: وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّى ** (15) and remembers the Name of his Lord, and prays?" The man replied: "Whenever He mentioned the Name of his Lord, he stood up and prayed." He (al-Rida) said: "Allah has surely burdened him with excess (شططا)." The man asked: "Then how is it?" He replied: "Whenever He mentioned the Name of his Lord, He invoked blessings (فَصَلّى) upon Muhammad and his Household (peace be upon them)."

footnote (١) And it was said that its meaning is that He makes easy for you, through kindnesses (*الألطاف*) and support (*التأييد*), that which establishes you upon your command and makes easy for you the difficult aspects of conveying the message (*تبليغ الرسالة*) and being patient upon it.

(١٦) بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا ** (16) Rather, you prefer the life of the world, And it was recited with the yāʾ (ي). (١٧) وَالْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقى ** (17) while the Hereafter is better and more lasting. For its bliss is pure from calamities (الغوائل) and has no cessation. (١٨) إِنَّ هذا لَفِي الصُّحُفِ الْأُولى. (18) All this is indeed in the former scriptures, (١٩) صُحُفِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى ** (19) the scriptures of Abraham and Moses. (peace be upon them) is a reference to what preceded, starting from His saying: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ.** (He is successful.)

In al-Khisal, narrated from Abu Dharr: He asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "How many books did Allah send down?" He replied: "One hundred books and four books. Allah sent down fifty scrolls (صحيفة) upon Seth (شيث), thirty scrolls upon Idris (peace be upon him), and twenty scrolls upon Abraham (peace be upon him). And He sent down the Torah (التوراة), the Gospel (الإنجيل), the Psalms (الزبور), and the Criterion (الفرقان)." He said: I asked: "O Messenger of Allah, what were the scriptures (صحف) of Abraham (peace be upon him)?" He said: "They were all parables (أمثال). And in them was: 'O afflicted, deceived king! I did not send you to gather the world (الدنيا), part of it upon another part, but I sent you so that you may repel from Me the supplication (دعوة) of the oppressed, for I do not reject it, even if it comes from a disbeliever (كافر).' And upon the wise person (العاقل), as long as he is not overwhelmed (مغلوب), is that he should have three hours: an hour in which he supplicates to his Lord (ربّه), an hour in which he holds himself accountable (يحاسب نفسه), and an hour in which he reflects upon what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has done for him. And an hour in which he is alone with his lawful (الحلال) share of pleasure, for this hour is an aid to those [other] hours, a rest for the hearts (القلوب), and a farewell (توديع) to them. And upon the wise person (العاقل) is that he should be insightful regarding his time (بصيراً بزمانه), attentive to his affair (مقبلاً على شأنه), and guarding his tongue (حافظاً للسانه), for whoever considers his speech to be part of his deeds (عمله), his speech will decrease, except in what concerns him (يعنيه). And upon the wise person (العاقل) is that he should be seeking three things: maintenance for a livelihood (مرمّة لمعاش), or provision for the return (تزوّد لمعاد), or enjoyment (تلذّذ) in what is not forbidden (محرّم)." He said: I asked: "O Messenger of Allah, what were the scriptures (صحف) of Moses?" He said: "They were all admonitions (عبراً). And in them was: 'Wondrous is he who is certain of death, yet how can he rejoice? And he who is certain of the Fire (النّار), yet how can he laugh? And he who sees the world (الدنيا) and its turning away from its people, yet how can he be content with it? And he who believes in Divine Decree (القدر), yet how can he toil (ينصب)? And he who is certain of the Reckoning (الحساب), yet does not act?'" He said: I asked: "Is there anything in our hands of what Allah sent down upon you that was in the scriptures (صحف) of Abraham and Moses (peace be upon them)?" He said: "O Abu Dharr, recite: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ تَزَكَّى (Successful is he who purifies himself) until the end of the Surah."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not give the Prophets anything except that He gave it to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." He said: "And He gave Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم what He gave the Prophets. And we possess the scriptures (الصحف) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned: صُحُفِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى ** (the scriptures of Abraham and Moses) (peace be upon them)." It was asked: "Are they the Tablets (الألواح)?" He said: "Yes."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Sabbih Isma Rabbika al-A'la (سبّح اسم ربّك الأعلى) in an obligatory prayer (فريضة) or a supererogatory prayer (نافلة), it will be said to him on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة): 'Enter Paradise (الجنّة) through whichever gates of Paradise you wish, if Allah wills.'" And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "It is obligatory (الواجب) upon every believer (مؤمن) who is a follower (شيعة) of ours that he recite al-Jumu'ah (الجمعة) and Sabbih Isma Rabbika al-A'la (سبّح اسم ربّك الأعلى) on the night of Friday (ليلة الجمعة)."
